items.note: Bürgenstock is a compliance room, not a peace summit: after Friday’s postponement, Pakistan says technical-level talks begin Sunday with Qatari mediators, and the MoU’s four ledgers — a 60-day clock, the Lebanon clause, no-charge Hormuz passage and a sanctions schedule — must now turn signatures into custody, inspection and timing.

Open only on someone’s spreadsheet: CENTCOM counted 55 ships and 17M+ barrels on June 20, AXS Marine put verified commercial crossings at 25 vs ~120 pre-war, and PetroChina could not fix a VLCC even near triple rates — a barrel can clear on price before a ship clears on risk.

The enforcement ledger is failing through violence: Reuters reported Israeli strikes killed at least 20 in Lebanon after the ceasefire took effect, with 50+ Hezbollah projectiles cited — and neither Israel nor Hezbollah signed the deal that is supposed to end their fighting.

The leak sits outside the perimeter: an unproven r/LocalLLaMA “Qwable-v1” distill of Fable 5 exposes the design flaw in frontier-model control — access can be revoked, traces cannot. Once outputs become a dataset and open weights train on them, the control crosses from revocable infrastructure into post-perimeter distribution.

items.what: Sunday technical-level talks are set, with an Iranian team led by Qalibaf and Araqchi and Vice President Vance expecting a couple of days focused on the nuclear file and the Lebanon ceasefire — instructions to be written, not a ceremony.

A US-brokered lull reduced the intensity of fighting without ending the dying — roughly nine killed including a child — with the process still stuck on the hard questions of disarmament and post-war governance.

Mona Khalil, the marine ecologist known as the "guardian of the turtles" of the southern coast, died of wounds from a June strike near Tyre — one named casualty inside a civilian toll the clause has not stopped.

China is tightening export checks on indium as AI hardware demand rises, adding another materials chokepoint to the compute build-out just as the access side hardens through export controls and bank compliance.
Markets are still pricing a possible October rate hike under Chair Warsh, keeping rate volatility live even as a lower oil tape eases the inflation read the other way.
